economic_notes: Nation has interest in Grand Rapids First Nation Development Corporation (grocery, gas bar, restaurant), Pinesiw Energy Limited Partnership (1/2 interest), Misipawistik Telecommunications Cooperative (internet), 10040628 Manitoba Ltd. (restaurant), Chus-Chug-U Gaming Trust (VLT lounge), Aseneskak Casino Limited Partnership (1/7 interest), Mahihkan Bus Lines Limited Partnership (1/6 interest), Grand Rapids First Nation Development Trust (funded by Manitoba Hydro settlement). Hydro claim against Canada for damages related to development on traditional territory is currently in arbitration.
notes: Going concern note flagged by auditor due to high demand loan debt of $32,638,228 at 5.19% variable interest rate. Nation has 4 fully consolidated subsidiaries (Housing Authority, Gaming Commission, Health Authority Inc., Lands Division) and 8 entities accounted for on modified equity basis including gaming, energy, telecom, transport, and trust entities. No budget figures presented for the year. Hydro claim against Canada in arbitration with potential financial and land settlement. Amortization in expenses by object ($2,624,415) differs slightly from Statement 3 ($2,485,962) — the difference may reflect GBE amortization. Community Development ($10,087,180) includes housing and capital projects but no separate housing expense line reported. Community Services ($2,012,914) covers infrastructure/policing/gaming regulation. No schedule of remuneration included in extracted text.
